message:
refactor(ffs-cli): extract repair and evidence commands into dedicated modules, add ffs-block tests

Move ~2300 lines from the monolithic main.rs into cmd_repair.rs (repair
orchestration, scrubbing, btrfs/ext4 recovery, superblock mirror repair,
staleness probing) and cmd_evidence.rs (evidence ledger filtering and
display). Promote shared types (RepairCommandOptions, RepairFlags,
RepairOutput, etc.) to pub visibility for cross-module access.

Add ~30 unit tests in ffs-block covering DirtyTracker (empty state,
mark/clear, re-mark, age ticks, flushable entries), MemoryPressure,
ARC cache state machine (pressure, trim, eviction, hit promotion,
scan-then-frequency, ghost list bounds, p-target bounds), AlignedVec,
NormalizedAlignment, BlockBuf COW semantics, CacheMetrics, and
CachePressureReport. Close beads issues bd-1irq, bd-1is8, bd-1isf,
bd-1isl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2r8","title":"Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation","description":"# Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation

## GOAL
Implement Cx-based budget system that propagates backpressure through the stack, preventing cascade failures under load.

## BACKGROUND (Alien-Artifact Quality)
Traditional systems fail abruptly under load. FrankenFS must degrade gracefully using information-theoretic principles:

1. **Budget = scarce resource allocation**
   - deadline: wall-clock timeout
   - poll_quota: async poll iterations allowed
   - cost_quota: abstract "work units" allowed
   - priority: relative importance

2. **Budget combination**: min for limits, max for priority

3. **Backpressure = signal to reduce demand**
   When budget exhausted, callers must either:
   - Retry with fresh budget (if transient)
   - Propagate failure (if permanent)
   - Shed load (if graceful degradation enabled)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Budget Types
```rust
pub struct Budget {
    pub deadline: Option<Instant>,
    pub poll_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub cost_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub priority: Priority,
}

impl Budget {
    pub fn combine(&self, other: &Budget) -> Budget {
        Budget {
            deadline: min_option(self.deadline, other.deadline),
            poll_quota: min_option(self.poll_quota, other.poll_quota),
            cost_quota: min_option(self.cost_quota, other.cost_quota),
            priority: max(self.priority, other.priority),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.deadline.map_or(false, |d| Instant::now() > d)
            || self.poll_quota == Some(0)
            || self.cost_quota == Some(0)
    }
}
```

### 2. Integration Points
- **FUSE dispatch**: Per-request budget from config
- **ARC cache**: Eviction budget (prevent eviction storms)
- **MVCC commit**: Transaction budget
- **Scrub/GC**: Background budget (yields to foreground)
- **RaptorQ decode**: Symbol budget

### 3. Pressure Detection
```rust
pub struct PressureMonitor {
    pub fn sample_cpu_load() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_memory_pressure() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_io_depth() -> usize;
    pub fn compute_degradation_level() -> DegradationLevel;
}
```

### 4. Graceful Degradation FSM
```
Normal -> Pause Background -> Reduce Cache -> Throttle Writes -> Read-Only
   ^                                                              |
   +--------------------(pressure relieved)----------------------+
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Budget combination semantics
2. Unit: Pressure detection thresholds
3. Integration: Simulated CPU load triggers degradation
4. Integration: Recovery when pressure relieved

## LOGGING
- Budget exhaustion (warn)
- Degradation level change (info)
- Pressure samples (trace)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Operations respect budget limits
2. Degradation level reflects actual pressure
3. Recovery is automatic and stable
4. No infinite loops or deadlocks under pressure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:09.209435721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741671654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741640536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","perf","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.370992350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.081031452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.977623160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Full CI green except pre-existing perf_regression_block_cache (hw-specific baseline).","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:09:44Z"},{"id":277,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"SunnyElm","text":"Unblocked all-features gate by fixing ffs-block S3-FIFO queue bookkeeping and perf regression in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs. Root cause was pending-admission key being demoted/evicted during miss-side rebalance, plus one-pass main-queue second-chance overflow and tiny-capacity split underfill. Implemented pending-admission protection in T1/T2 rebalance, emergency clean-eviction fallback with dirty-overflow tolerance, tiny-capacity split relaxation, and bounded fast-hit promotion strategy to restore lookup_p99. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --lib; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --test perf_regression_e2e -- --nocapture; cargo test --workspace --all-features all passing.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:11:30Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2s4","title":"EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime","description":"# EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime

## PURPOSE
Transform the FUSE interface from scaffold to production-ready mount runtime, as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gap #3.

## BACKGROUND
From spec §14 (FUSE Integration):
- Multi-threaded dispatch required
- All state must be Send + Sync
- Never block FUSE dispatch thread on raw I/O
- Proper lifecycle management (mount/unmount/signal handling)

Current state: "FUSE mount runtime: ❌ Interface scaffold only"

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Lifecycle
- Clean mount with explicit options (ro/rw, cache_size, etc.)
- Graceful unmount with flush and cleanup
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT -> clean unmount)
- Crash recovery on restart

### 2. Multi-threaded Dispatch
- Use fuser multi-threaded mode
- Thread pool sizing based on CPU count
- Per-thread ARC cache access (Send + Sync)
- Connection pooling for concurrent requests

### 3. Error Handling
- Every FUSE op maps to correct errno
- Timeout handling for hung operations
- Partial failure recovery

### 4. Performance
- Request batching where applicable
- Readahead for sequential access
- Writeback coalescing
- Attribute caching with configurable TTL

### 5. Observability
- Per-op latency metrics
- Request queue depth
- Error rate tracking

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount --rw /path/to/image.ext4 /mnt/ffs` works in production
2. Concurrent readers/writers do not deadlock
3. `umount /mnt/ffs` is clean (no data loss)
4. SIGTERM results in clean shutdown
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping

## FILES
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs (main implementation)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/mount.rs (lifecycle)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/dispatch.rs (multi-thread)
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (mount command)

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E suite (bd-3en1) passes
3. ffs mount --rw works on ext4 and btrfs images
4. SIGTERM unmount is clean (no data loss)
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:01.826764621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072572592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072549739Z","close_reason":"Completed: all child beads closed and acceptance criteria validated by latest E2E artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","fuse","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":115,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: runtime_backpressure
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.5 Graceful Degradation + 10.3 Learning-Augmented Online + 12.13 Change-point
EV score: 16 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: queue_budget=1024, throttle_sleep_ms<=25, shed_ratio_cap=0.15
Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:17Z"},{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coordination note: claiming child bead bd-cebm for fsync/flush durability path work now. Implemented initial fsync/flush/fsyncdir wiring and durability sync behavior for ext4 path with passing full workspace gates; next pass should deepen integration tests/perf proof against the bead acceptance criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:44:11Z"},{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Child bd-cebm progress: durability + proof artifacts expanded with fsync crash-boundary checks and fsync latency benchmarks; workspace gates passing. Latest deterministic run artifacts: artifacts/e2e/20260218_042005_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log (PASSED, duration 9s) and artifacts/e2e/20260218_042005_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/sigkill_crash/recovery_verify_report.json (errors=[]; baseline_verified=500; fsynced_records=25). Acceptance criteria satisfied: production rw mount path validated, clean unmount/remount persistence validated, SIGKILL recovery validated, errno path and lifecycle tests already closed in child beads.","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:37Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ha8","title":"Fix btrfs dotdot resolution, add INODE_REF parent backref, truncation extent cleanup","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T22:08:00.324701299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T22:43:19.901540120Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T22:43:19.901516956Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3hi","title":"Docs: Align errno mapping (Cancelled EINTR vs ECANCELED, etc.)","description":"Problem: COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md currently documents Cancelled -> EINTR, but crates/ffs-error/src/lib.rs maps Cancelled -> ECANCELED.

Goal: choose the canonical errno semantics for each FfsError variant (especially cancellation) and ensure:
- ffs-error::to_errno() matches that choice
- docs tables match that choice
- FUSE behavior uses it consistently

Acceptance:
- A single authoritative errno mapping table exists (prefer ffs-error docs or code comment) and docs reference it.
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC + PLAN + PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE do not contradict the code.
- We document the rationale (EINTR vs ECANCELED) in one place.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:34.660731378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:01.225330808Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:01.225311371Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hi","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:05.597011413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3hr","title":"ffs-error: Decide Cancelled errno semantics and update to_errno()","description":"Context: cancellation propagates via asupersync::Cx::checkpoint(). For FUSE, cancellation can be represented as EINTR (interrupted) or ECANCELED (explicit cancellation).

 {"id":"bd-3ib","title":"EPIC: Performance Optimization","description":"# EPIC: Performance Optimization

## PURPOSE
Establish performance baselines and optimize hot paths following the "Extreme Software Optimization" methodology.

## BACKGROUND
From AGENTS.md and the extreme-software-optimization methodology:
- Profile first
- One optimization lever per commit
- Prove behavior unchanged (goldens or invariants)
- Isomorphism proof template required

This epic should never run ahead of conformance infrastructure: performance claims must be backed by reproducible baselines and behavior proofs.

## METHODOLOGY (MANDATORY)

### The Loop
```
1. BASELINE  -> hyperfine (record JSON)
2. PROFILE   -> flamegraph/perf
3. PROVE     -> goldens + invariants (sha256sum, unit tests)
4. CHANGE    -> one lever per commit (Score >= 2.0)
5. VERIFY    -> gates + golden verification
6. REPEAT
```

### Opportunity Matrix Template
| Hotspot | Impact (1-5) | Confidence (1-5) | Effort (1-5) | Score |
|---------|--------------|------------------|--------------|-------|
| func:line | x | x | / | Impact*Conf/Effort |

Rule: Only implement Score >= 2.0

### Isomorphism Proof Template (per change)
```
## Change: [description]
- Ordering preserved:     [yes/no + why]
- Tie-breaking unchanged: [yes/no + why]
- Floating-point:         N/A
- RNG seeds:              N/A
- Goldens:                ./scripts/verify_golden.sh ✓
```

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Hyperfine baselines exist for the supported CLI + mount smoke workflows
2. Flamegraphs generated and analyzed
3. Opportunity matrix populated
4. At least 3 optimizations implemented with behavior proofs
5. No performance regressions detected vs baseline

## DEPENDENCIES
- EPIC: Conformance & Quality Infrastructure (bd-2jk)

## EXPLICIT NON-GOALS
- Premature optimization before profiling
- Micro-optimizations with Score < 2.0
- Any change without an isomorphism proof + golden verification

## Success Criteria
1. Performance work is reproducible: a developer can re-run baseline + profile scripts and get comparable results.
2. Every optimization includes an explicit proof of unchanged behavior (goldens/invariants) and a measured delta.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:09.695775688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T19:53:49.782975350Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T19:53:49.782914817Z","close_reason":"All children completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf"],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-3ib","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ib.1","title":"Profile read path and generate flamegraph","description":"# Profile read path and generate flamegraph

## GOAL
Generate CPU flamegraphs for the real user-facing read paths so perf work is evidence-driven (no guesswork).

## PRECONDITIONS
- Baselines are recorded (bd-2jk.3).
- Behavior proof gate exists: `./scripts/verify_golden.sh` must pass before/after any optimization.

## CURRENT REALITY (PATHS)
Use canonical repo paths:
- ext4 reference image: `conformance/golden/ext4_8mb_reference.ext4`
- benchmark entrypoint: `scripts/benchmark.sh`
- E2E smoke: `scripts/e2e/ffs_smoke.sh`

## PROFILE TARGETS
1. CLI inspect path:
   - `ffs-cli inspect <ext4_reference> --json`
2. FUSE read path:
   - mount ext4 RO
   - read a file through the mount (bounded `dd`)

## IMPLEMENTATION (PHASED)
### Phase A: CLI flamegraph (required)
1. Install tools (local dev only):
   - `cargo install flamegraph`
   - ensure `perf` works on your machine (some systems require adjusting perf permissions)
2. Profile:
   - `cargo flamegraph -p ffs-cli -- inspect conformance/golden/ext4_8mb_reference.ext4 --json > /dev/null`
3. Save artifacts:
   - `profiles/flamegraph_cli_inspect.svg`

### Phase B: FUSE read flamegraph (recommended)
1. Mount RO in one terminal:
   - `cargo run -p ffs-cli --release -- mount conformance/golden/ext4_8mb_reference.ext4 /tmp/ffs-mnt`
2. Record read workload in another terminal:
   - `perf record -g -- dd if=/tmp/ffs-mnt/readme.txt of=/dev/null bs=4k count=1000`
3. Render flamegraph (example pipeline):
   - `perf script | stackcollapse-perf.pl | flamegraph.pl > profiles/flamegraph_fuse_read.svg`

## OUTPUT (CHECK IN THE ANALYSIS, NOT NECESSARILY ALL SVGs)
Create/update `PROFILE_ANALYSIS.md` with:
- profile date + commit SHA
- hardware + kernel + tool versions
- top hotspots table
- opportunity matrix (Impact/Confidence/Effort/Score)
- recommended first optimization target(s)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. CLI inspect flamegraph exists and hotspots are documented.
2. (If FUSE available) FUSE read flamegraph exists and hotspots are documented.
3. Opportunity matrix populated and ties back to baseline numbers.
4. `./scripts/verify_golden.sh` passes before/after profiling runs (sanity).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:23.178997306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:40:40.005343458Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:40:40.005317600Z","close_reason":"Added PROFILE_ANALYSIS.md with CLI flamegraph artifact metadata, hotspot table, baseline tie-in, and opportunity matrix; verified with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-gates scripts/verify_golden.sh","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ib.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.634264780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ib.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:29.697380738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ib.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:23.178997306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ib.2","title":"Graveyard alien CS: thread-per-core dispatch for FUSE (Graveyard Entry 15.7)","description":"# Graveyard alien CS: thread-per-core dispatch for FUSE (Graveyard Entry 15.7)

## GOAL
Investigate thread-per-core architecture for FUSE dispatch, replacing the shared-nothing thread pool. From alien_cs_graveyard.md entry on thread-per-core architecture.

## BACKGROUND
Thread-per-core (Seastar/Glommio model) eliminates cross-core synchronization for I/O dispatch. FrankenFS FUSE dispatch currently uses fuser's multi-threaded mode with shared state. Thread-per-core would assign each FUSE fd to a specific core with core-local caches and per-core WAL buffers (see bd-2oah).

## GRAVEYARD INTEGRATION
- References: alien_cs_graveyard.md thread-per-core entry
- Failure modes documented in graveyard: work imbalance, cross-core joins, debugging difficulty
- Adoption wedge: benchmark showing per-core dispatch vs shared-state dispatch on 4+ cores

## DELIVERABLES
1. Design doc: thread-per-core dispatch architecture for ffs-fuse
2. Prototype: core-pinned FUSE dispatch with per-core ARC cache partition
3. Benchmark: throughput comparison at 4, 8, 16 cores
4. Decision: adopt or reject based on measured improvement

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Benchmark: 4K random read IOPS, thread-per-core vs current
2. Benchmark: sequential write throughput, thread-per-core vs current
3. Correctness: concurrent operations produce same results as shared-state

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Core assignment (info): fuse_fd, core_id
- Cross-core request (debug): source_core, target_core, op_type
- Per-core stats (info): core_id, ops_completed, cache_hit_rate

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Design doc with measured tradeoffs
2. Prototype compiles and passes basic tests
3. Benchmark data for go/no-go decision
4. Graveyard failure modes addressed or acknowledged","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:07.767992853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:59:55.713397345Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:59:55.713327414Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","fuse","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ib.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:07.767992853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"bd-3ib.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Budgeted mode: queue_budget=1024, throttle_sleep_ms<=25, shed_ratio_cap=0.15
Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:

- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-g30","title":"Track: CLI + TUI (ffs-cli + ffs-tui)","description":"Provide operator tooling: CLI subcommands (inspect/info/fsck/mount), and a frankentui-based dashboard to observe cache/MVCC/repair stats.\n\nAcceptance: CLI is the primary UX for harness + debugging; TUI can attach to a running mount to show internal metrics.
